friction_items.description: 226 tools in TOOL_DEFINITIONS at boot; 36 are in the 'audit' category alone. From a maintainer triage lens the catalog is past the point where a human can hold the surface in mental working memory. The semantic search/sys_active_tools narrowing helps the AI, but the maintainer's own debugging cost grows linearly: a bug report saying 'audit failed' could be in any of 36 tools. The engineering benchmark itself only needs ~12 tools, but the surface the AI must filter through is 19x larger.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Consolidate audit tools behind a single dispatcher (tool_audit with kind='claims'|'figure'|'reproducibility'|...) and let tool_audit_quality_full continue to be the meta-runner. Drop alias-only tools whose original wasn't deprecated. Target: <150 in TOOL_DEFINITIONS by 2.0.

friction_items.description: Routing 'benchmark these three sorting algorithms at sizes 1k/10k/100k/1M/10M, 10 runs each' lands in methodology/method_comparison — an ML-flavored protocol with a single 'engineering_or_systems_benchmark_addendum' step buried as step 9 of 10. A maintainer reviewing the routing decision sees the right protocol matched but for the wrong vocabulary, and the engineering pack does NOT contribute a benchmark protocol of its own. The addendum-as-step pattern is an anti-pattern: it nests the engineering doctrine inside an ML scaffold the engineer didn't ask for.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a first-class engineering/test/algorithmic_benchmark protocol to the engineering pack (warm-up, governor pinning, log-log scaling fit, Wilcoxon paired test, language-stdlib baseline). Move the existing addendum's content into it. Have method_comparison cross-reference the engineering protocol when the task vocabulary is engineering-flavored, instead of hosting an addendum step.

friction_items.description: No first-class 'run grid' / 'benchmark harness' tool. A 3 algorithms x 5 sizes x 10 runs sweep = 150 invocations that the researcher orchestrates manually with nested loops in one of the scripts/<NN>_<method>.py files. There's no tool_grid_runner, tool_benchmark_harness, or tool_replicate_runs primitive. The build_test_fix_loop protocol tracks cycle times for ONE iteration — not a parametric sweep. Maintainer concern: this is the single most common engineering ask and it has no primitive.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add tool_engineering_benchmark_harness(spec_path) that takes a YAML {methods: [...], sizes: [...], n_runs: N, warmup: K, governor: 'performance'} and emits a long-form CSV of (method, size, run_idx, wall_clock_ms, peak_rss, exit_code) suitable for ggplot/seaborn faceting. Pair with tool_engineering_scaling_fit that does the log-log exponent + CI.

friction_items.description: The engineering pack's _err() helper returns {status: 'error', error: <message>}. No error_code, no suggested_next_action, no link to docs. When fmea_render fails because the YAML doesn't have items: at top level, the user sees 'FMEA spec must contain a list of items' but nothing tells them the exact key name expected at the spec root or which protocol step they were on. A maintainer debugging a user issue from a log can't grep by error_code.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add error_code (snake_case stable identifier) + suggested_action fields to both _ok/_err envelopes across all pack tools. Codify the envelope in research_os.plugins so packs inherit it instead of redefining.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_reproducibility re-runs EVERY experiment script in a clean env. For 150 benchmark runs (each script potentially expensive: a 10M-element sort might be 30s) this is a 75+ minute audit. There's no 'sample N% of runs', 'reproduce only the headline numbers', or 'budgeted reproducibility audit' mode. A maintainer reviewing CI logs will see the audit time-out before it returns a verdict and the AI will retry, doubling the cost.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add reproducibility_audit_budget_s and sample_strategy ('all'|'random_k'|'extremes_only'|'headline_only') parameters. Default to 'headline_only' when n_runs > 20. Persist the chosen strategy in workspace/audits/reproducibility_audit_plan.md so the maintainer can see WHY only a subset was rerun.

friction_items.description: tool_reliability_log_event exists but the engineering protocols never call it. A maintainer trying to answer 'how often does the build_test_fix_loop's diagnose_dominance gate fire?' has no data — the protocol does the math in the AI's head and writes a markdown file but doesn't emit a structured event. tool_reliability_report has nothing to aggregate for engineering work.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a 'telemetry:' block to each engineering protocol naming the events it should emit (e.g. 'cycle.closed', 'cycle.diagnose_dominated', 'cycle.same_failure_third_time'). Have the protocol_completion injector also remind the AI to flush a tool_reliability_log_event with the named event_type.

friction_items.description: tool_step_complete vs tool_audit_step_completeness vs tool_audit_step_literature — three tools whose names rhyme. tool_synthesize vs tool_synthesize_plan vs tool_synthesis_preview vs tool_synthesis_curate_figures — four 'synthes-' tools. A maintainer reading a transcript can't always tell which one the AI called without inspecting args. Compounded by 226-tool surface.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Audit naming with a grep -E pattern on each shared prefix; rename outliers. e.g. tool_audit_step_completeness → tool_audit_step (kind='completeness'|'literature'). tool_synthesize_plan → tool_plan_synthesis (matches tool_plan_advance / tool_plan_turn cluster).

friction_items.description: engineering/test/build_test_fix_loop's editorial_voice rules are good prose but heavy: 'A cycle without a recorded duration is not a cycle; it is a vibe.' 'The diagnose phase is the most lied-about phase.' Maintainer concern: these read as opinionated lectures that a senior researcher might find condescending. The substance is right; the tone may not survive contact with users outside the original author's circle.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Keep the substance, soften two of the four rules. Rule-as-aphorism works once per protocol; four in a row reads as preachy.

friction_items.description: Step ID 'engineering_or_systems_benchmark_addendum' inside method_comparison.yaml is verbose and hard to grep. The 'addendum' framing tells future maintainers this content is second-class.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Once the new engineering/test/algorithmic_benchmark protocol exists, delete this step from method_comparison.yaml and replace with a one-line cross-link in description.

friction_items.description: If tool_audit_quality_full fires during the benchmark synthesis and one of its 36 sub-checks fails, the maintainer (or AI) has to inspect a long JSON to identify which sub-check + which file + which rule was violated. There is no terse 'top blocker + one fix suggestion' summary path.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a top_blocker field to tool_audit_quality_full's response with the highest-severity failed check + a one-line remediation hint.

confusing_protocols: methodology/method_comparison (ML-flavored but the engineering benchmark addendum is buried as step 9)
engineering/test/build_test_fix_loop (overlapping triggers with engineering/test/test_failure_causation; aphoristic editorial voice is heavy)
engineering/test/test_failure_causation (could route ahead of build_test_fix_loop on 'test failure' triggers; precedence in adversarial cases unclear)
recommendations: Add a first-class engineering/test/algorithmic_benchmark protocol + a tool_engineering_benchmark_harness primitive. Move the systems-benchmark addendum from method_comparison.yaml into it. This is the most common engineering ask and currently has no native home.

Consolidate the 36-tool audit category. Target: collapse 6-8 step-related audit tools behind tool_audit_step(kind=...); collapse 4 figure audit tools behind tool_audit_figure(kind=...). Target audit category size: <20.
Add error_code + suggested_action to every pack tool's _err envelope. Maintainer-grepability is a hard requirement once external users start filing bug reports.
Add reproducibility_audit_budget_s + sample_strategy to tool_audit_reproducibility. The 150-run benchmark exposes the no-budget design as untenable.
Add per-protocol telemetry: section that names the event_types the AI should flush via tool_reliability_log_event. Without this, tool_reliability_report has nothing to aggregate for engineering work.
Rename the tool_step_* + tool_synthesize* clusters to disambiguate by kind= parameter rather than by suffix. Cuts the 226-tool surface without removing functionality.
Add a top_blocker + remediation_hint field to tool_audit_quality_full's response.

Document the routing precedence between engineering/test/build_test_fix_loop and engineering/test/test_failure_causation explicitly in the engineering pack README — or unify them under a single test_loop protocol with branching steps.
